findings.title: Provider resolution forces local-subprocess; active backend never detected
findings.summary: resolveFreshSessionProviderName defaults to 'local-subprocess' and only env-sniffs when provider==='auto', so claude-code/Claude Desktop (and the other backends) are never detected by the conversation-first flow.
evidence: meta-audit-log Cause 1: session-config pins provider:'local-subprocess'; resolveFreshSessionProviderName (providers/index.ts:46-50) returns the requested provider verbatim unless it is literally 'auto'. Verified in code during intake.
classifyProvider('local-subprocess') -> 'local' selects the first-contact ceiling of 3 (limits.ts:11-13).
User Q-002: wants detection across claude-code, claude-desktop, codex, vscode, opencode, antigravity.

findings.title: Canonical dispatch path never acquires live quota (central regression)
findings.summary: prepareDispatchArtifacts/cmdPrepareDispatch passes hostModel:null and never builds a QuotaSource, queries usage, or threads a snapshot to the scheduler, so the scheduler's remaining-quota logic is unreachable from the conversation-first flow.
evidence: meta-audit-log Cause 2 (central): the canonical prepareDispatchArtifacts path reads only the on-disk cache via lookupDiscoveredLimits; never calls provider.queryLimits, never constructs a QuotaSource, never queries current usage, never passes quotaSourceSnapshot. The legacy in-process path (cli.ts:~2030-2056) does all of this.
scheduler.ts:168-177 (remaining_pct < 0.1 -> wave 1; < 0.3 -> halve) is therefore unreachable from the flow CLAUDE.md calls canonical.
Verified during intake: prepareDispatchArtifacts (cli/dispatch.ts:321) accepts hostModel but its body loads the bundle and builds packets without quota acquisition.

findings.title: No provider implements real quota querying (queryLimits is a stub)
findings.summary: queryLimits is declared optional but implemented by zero of five providers; implement real best-effort remaining-quota querying for claude-code, claude-desktop, opencode, and vscode (per-backend feasibility varies).
evidence: meta-audit-log Cause 3: FreshSessionProvider.queryLimits is optional (shared/providers/types.ts:45) and implemented by zero providers. Verified: the symbol appears only in the interface and the legacy caller (cli.ts).
meta-audit-log Cause 4: probeProvider is @deprecated and returns supported:false for all providers except an unimplemented subprocess-template.
User Q-002: 'I want real quota querying ... if possible' -- best-effort per backend.

findings.title: Add a cascading quota-signal fallback chain
findings.summary: Compose a first-class fallback cascade (provider queryLimits -> discovered/cached -> reactive learned -> model-based default -> conservative provider/local default) so the scheduler always receives the best available signal and degrades gracefully on first contact.
evidence: meta-audit-log Cause 4: the only dynamic source (LearnedQuotaSource) is reactive and empty on first contact; there is no proactive acquisition at plan time.
User Q-002: 'with cascading fallbacks (model-based, provider-based, etc.)'.
Constraint: graceful degradation -- absence of a signal must never throw; first contact is normal.

findings.title: Add Codex and Antigravity providers (resolution + best-effort quota)
findings.summary: Add codex and antigravity as providers conforming to FreshSessionProvider, participating in provider resolution and best-effort quota querying; a distinct claude-desktop classification if it differs from claude-code.
evidence: User Q-002 names codex and antigravity, which are NOT current providers (current set: claude-code, opencode, subprocess-template, vscode-task, local-subprocess).
Minimum obligation per the brief: provider resolution + best-effort quota; full audit-worker dispatch parity is a per-backend planning decision.

findings.title: Windows path-join bug produces malformed artifacts directory name
findings.summary: Artifacts-dir/repo-root resolution concatenated 'C:\Code' + 'auditor-lambda' without a separator, yielding 'Codeauditor-lambda.audit-artifacts'; locate and fix the path-construction site and add a Windows path test.

affected_files.path: DISCOVERY TARGET: artifacts-dir / repo-root resolution (audit-code wrapper or src/io)

evidence: meta-audit-log Secondary observation: 'packages/audit-code/Codeauditor-lambda.audit-artifacts/session-config.json' looks like a path-join bug ('C:\Code' + 'auditor-lambda' concatenated without a separator).
Repo mandate is explicitly 'Windows-aware'.
Intake check: the stray directory is not currently on disk (since cleaned), so the code site must be located rather than reproduced from an artifact.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md audit priority chain drifted from live PRIORITY[]
findings.summary: CLAUDE.md's documented audit priority chain omits three obligations present in the live PRIORITY[] (graph_enrichment_current, design_assessment_current, design_review_completed); reconcile, ideally with a test that fails on divergence.
evidence: meta-audit-log O6: CLAUDE.md documents the priority chain but the live PRIORITY[] in nextStep.ts has three extra obligations (graph_enrichment_current, design_assessment_current, design_review_completed).
Noted as a smell that becomes a real finding (DR-007).

findings.title: Add a single-worker canary before the dispatch fan-out
findings.summary: Run and verify one packet's submit round-trip before launching the full fan-out, so a submit misconfiguration is caught after one worker instead of wasting whole waves.
evidence: meta-audit-log O8/O9: all 106 workers are dispatched before learning whether even one submit-packet command can run; a single-worker canary would de-risk the most expensive step.
DR-004: a broken hardcoded submit path already exists in this repo, making the canary concretely valuable.

findings.title: Add a sampling / coverage-budget (top-K) dispatch mode with honest partial reporting
findings.summary: Offer a top-K (by risk/priority) packet mode that synthesizes an honest PARTIAL report instead of the all-or-loop-back 'dispatch every packet' stop condition; partial coverage must be represented truthfully in coverage_matrix and synthesis.
evidence: meta-audit-log O8/O10: stop_condition is 'Dispatch every packet'; there is no first-class 'audit the top-K risk packets and synthesize a partial report' mode, a steep on-ramp for a quick read of a 41k-LOC repo.

findings.title: Add a human-in-the-loop confirmation before large fan-out
findings.summary: Before launching a large dispatch, surface a 'this will launch N agents across M waves -- continue?' gate so the host pauses before the dominant-cost step.

affected_files.path: DISCOVERY TARGET: /audit-code loader / host-asset source templates

evidence: meta-audit-log O10: 106 deep subagents is a ~15x larger spend than a high-value subset and was not anticipated by 'run the skill'; a human-in-the-loop 'launch N agents, continue?' confirmation would belong in the loader.

findings.title: Anchor loader commands to an explicit cwd
findings.summary: Make the /audit-code (and /remediate-code) loader use absolute paths or an explicit cwd anchor so bare relative commands stop breaking against stateful shell cwd.

affected_files.path: DISCOVERY TARGET: /audit-code & /remediate-code loader / host-asset source templates

evidence: meta-audit-log O2: the loader runs bare 'node audit-code.mjs next-step' assuming cwd == package dir; a persisted cd caused a doubled path and MODULE_NOT_FOUND. 'The single sharpest footgun so far.'

findings.title: Echo resolved scope before writing artifacts
findings.summary: Before creating .audit-artifacts, echo the resolved scope ('auditing X, N files, git: yes/no -- proceed?') so a mis-resolved repo_root (e.g. a sub-package) is caught before artifacts are written.
evidence: meta-audit-log O3: a cwd accident silently re-scoped the audit to 1 of 3 workspaces and disabled git diff signals; nothing forced scope confirmation before artifacts were written. A confirmation/echo would catch this class of error.

findings.title: Give design_review a bounded effort budget
findings.summary: Add a reading budget or 'focus on the top-N risk units' hint to the design_review step so its open-ended 'read the project source' instruction does not become unbounded effort on large repos.

affected_files.path: DISCOVERY TARGET: design_review step-prompt template / packages/audit-code/src/orchestrator

evidence: meta-audit-log O5: design_review is bounded in output but unbounded in input effort ('read the project source' over 511 files/41k LOC); no budget or prioritized reading list. A suggested file budget or top-N risk-unit hint would calibrate effort.

findings.title: Point findings output at a machine-validatable JSON Schema
findings.summary: Reference the AuditResult JSON Schema from the step prompt so a host can self-validate its output before submit, instead of receiving validation feedback late on ingest.
evidence: meta-audit-log O7: the schema is restated in prose rather than pointed at a JSON Schema file, so a host can't machine-validate its own output before submitting (it's validated on ingest instead -- late feedback).
